name: Snyder
description: An early advanced method created by Anthony Snyder in 1982. The Snyder method starts with a 2x2x2 block, expands into a complete first two layers, then finishes with a custom last layer approach. It was ahead of its time in using blockbuilding concepts and influenced later methods. The method aims for a low move count through efficient F2L construction and specialized last layer algorithms.

steps.name: 2x2x2 Block
steps.description: Build a 2x2x2 block in any corner of the cube. This is done intuitively by pairing a corner with its three adjacent edges and building outward.
tips: Plan the entire block during inspection
Color neutrality allows choosing the easiest block
Look for pre-formed pairs in the scramble

steps.name: F2L Completion
steps.description: Expand the 2x2x2 block to complete the entire first two layers. This involves solving the remaining corners and edges of the bottom two layers using a combination of blockbuilding and pair insertion techniques.
tips: Extend the block into a 2x2x3 first when possible
Use efficient pair insertions to minimize moves
Plan transitions to avoid cube rotations

steps.name: Custom Last Layer
steps.description: Solve the last layer using Snyder's specialized algorithm set. The method uses a unique approach to the last layer that can involve partial or full one-look solutions depending on the case recognized. Snyder developed custom algorithms optimized for move count.
tips: Snyder's LL approach predates modern OLL/PLL standardization
COLL can be used as a modern substitute for corner cases
Recognition requires practice with the custom case set

cons: Custom last layer algorithms are not widely documented
Not competitive with modern optimized methods
Almost no community support or development
creator: Anthony Snyder
